SOURCES SOUGHT / STATEMENT OF CAPABILITY — Sample response (8(a) small business construction)

Reference: Statement of Capability to Provide Construction Services for Design-Build Renovation and Initial Outfitting Supporting the Renovation of a Tyndall AFB Dental Clinic (USACE District, Little Rock; Solicitation #W9127S13R6012).

Structure of a strong Sources Sought / capability response:

Opening: identify the firm (a Miami-based 8(a) and Small Business Certified contractor), state intent to provide professional services for the referenced project, and note relevant track record — e.g. "successfully completed more than 50 construction contracts of varied scope and size since acceptance into the SBA 8(a) program in 2007," specializing in design-build and metal building construction, building renovations, specialty construction, and project management; 100% of jobs performed on time and within budget, often ahead of schedule, with zero past-performance complaints.

Submittal requirements addressed in order:
1. Submittal Intention — confirm interest and that a qualified team of professionals has been assembled with extensive experience in the design and construction of similar projects.
2. Company Profile — number of employees; office location; single bonding capacity (e.g. $1,500,000); aggregate bonding capacity (e.g. $6,000,000); DUNS/UEI; CAGE code; small business designation/status claimed (8(a) Certified); responsible office contact person (name, title — Senior PM/Construction Manager — phone, email).
3. Relevant Experience — for each past project list: Role (Prime or Subcontractor), Contract Value, Point of Contact, Timeliness of Performance (finished on/ahead of schedule), Customer Satisfaction (zero callbacks/warranty issues), and Scope of work. Examples: install metal aircraft shelters/sunshades with full electrical; design-build fire-evacuation egress stairways; interior renovations (VCT/flooring/paint of hangar bays and offices); commander's office renovations (gypsum board, ceilings, flooring, cabinets, rewire); concrete repair and gutter systems; flight control tower window replacement (impact windows across 12 stories); perimeter chain-link fence with cable barrier and barbed wire; multi-building mold remediation (metal roof, SPF attic insulation, EIFS, masonry).

Teaching point: a winning Sources Sought / SoC response mirrors the solicitation's submittal checklist exactly, leads with set-aside status + bonding capacity, and proves past performance with quantified scope, contract values, and named POCs who will vouch for on-time, zero-defect delivery.
